title: China-to-Algeria Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: CREG Regulatory Framework and Sonelgaz Grid-Connection Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Algeria's electricity and gas sector is regulated by CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Electricité et du Gaz), established under Law 02-01 on electricity and gas distribution. Sonelgaz (Société Nationale de l'Electricité et du Gaz) is the incumbent utility and distribution network operator; OS (Opérateur du Système) manages the national transmission grid and grid codes, with GRTE (Gestionnaire du Réseau de Transport de l'Electricité) responsible for the transmission network. Grid-connected PV inverters must comply with Sonelgaz technical connection specifications covering voltage and frequency ride-through, reactive-power management, anti-islanding protection, reconnection delay, power-quality requirements, and metering. Large-scale solar projects must obtain a generation licence from CREG. Algeria's Loi 04-09 (relative à la promotion des énergies renouvelables dans le cadre du développement durable) and subsequent decrees establish the legal basis for renewable energy generation and grid access.
target_standards: Loi 02-01 relative à l'électricité et à la distribution du gaz par canalisations (electricity and gas distribution law)
Loi 04-09 relative à la promotion des énergies renouvelables dans le cadre du développement durable
Décrets exécutifs implementing Loi 04-09 (renewable energy production, grid access)
Sonelgaz technical grid-connection specifications for distributed and utility-scale PV generation
OS / GRTE grid code for the Algerian transmission system
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection evidence for PV inverters commonly cites GB/T 19964-2024 (technical requirements for PV power stations connecting to power systems) and GB/T 37408-2021. These standards address similar technical areas but are written for the Chinese 50 Hz grid and regulatory framework; they are not acceptable as Sonelgaz grid-connection documentation or CREG project-approval evidence for Algeria.
rows.gap: Prepare an Algeria-specific Sonelgaz grid settings sheet covering: voltage and frequency ride-through windows for the 50 Hz Algerian grid (230 V single phase / 400 V three phase), reactive-power Q(U) and Q(P) behaviour, anti-islanding method, reconnection delay, active-power curtailment interface, protection relay settings, metering point, and power-quality compliance. Obtain a generation licence from CREG before construction for projects above the distributed-generation threshold. Confirm Loi 04-09 registration and authorisation requirements with CREG and the Ministry of Energy for the applicable capacity tier.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected PV inverters under Sonelgaz technical rules; CREG generation licence is mandatory for utility-scale projects under Loi 02-01 and Loi 04-09
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for Chinese grid-connected PV projects under CNCA and NB/T 32004; not transferable as Sonelgaz grid approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 19964-2024 grid documentation does not satisfy Sonelgaz grid-connection approval or CREG licensing requirements. Prepare Algeria-specific Sonelgaz settings and obtain the required CREG generation licence before commissioning.

source.publisher: Commission de Régulation de l'Electricité et du Gaz (CREG), Algeria

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Frequency and Voltage Settings for Algeria
rows.target_requirement: Algeria operates a 50 Hz grid with low-voltage nominal voltage of 230 V (single phase) and 400 V (three phase). The Algerian grid is synchronised with the Maghreb regional interconnection (COMELEC / RAEC / ELTAM) covering Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia, and Libya, which connects to the European ENTSO-E synchronous area via links to Spain and Italy. Inverters must be configured for 50 Hz operation with frequency ride-through, trip thresholds, reconnection delay, and reactive-power behaviour following Sonelgaz / OS technical rules and IEC practice. Chinese GB/T firmware defaults calibrated for the Chinese 50 Hz grid may differ in frequency and voltage tolerance bands from Sonelgaz requirements.
target_standards: Sonelgaz / OS technical grid-connection specifications for distributed and utility-scale PV
IEC 61727 (PV systems — characteristics of the utility interface)
IEC 62116 (utility-interconnected PV inverters — test procedure for islanding prevention measures)
ENTSO-E / Maghreb COMELEC regional grid code — relevant for larger plants
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverters default to settings under GB/T 37408-2021 and GB/T 19964-2024 for the Chinese 50 Hz grid. While the nominal frequency matches Algeria, trip windows, reconnection sequences, reactive-power curve shapes, and active-power response ramps may differ from Sonelgaz or IEC expectations. Chinese inverters typically support multi-market grid profiles; firmware parameter adjustment for the Algeria market is necessary.
rows.gap: Confirm that the inverter model supports an Algeria / Maghreb 50 Hz grid profile. Provide Sonelgaz or the project engineer with a firmware settings sheet showing voltage and frequency trip thresholds, reconnection delay, reactive-power curve, and any active-power reduction ramp. Do not ship with factory-default China-only settings without verification against Sonelgaz grid requirements.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory parameter compliance for grid connection under Sonelgaz / OS technical rules; firmware settings are a project-commissioning requirement
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory under Chinese grid-connection inspection and acceptance; China settings are not directly transferable to Algeria

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] 50 Hz frequency is shared between China and Algeria, but grid parameter windows differ. Provide a Sonelgaz-compatible settings sheet and confirm inverter firmware supports the Algerian grid profile before commissioning.

source.publisher: Sonelgaz (Société Nationale de l'Electricité et du Gaz), Algeria

rows.requirement: Algeria Solar Tender Programme and CREG / Ministry of Energy Market Access
rows.target_requirement: Algeria has committed to large-scale solar capacity expansion through successive tender programmes. The Solar 1000 programme and follow-on tenders targeting 3 000–4 000 MW of solar PV capacity are managed by CREG and the Ministry of Energy and Mining. These tenders set project-specific technical specifications — typically referencing IEC standards for inverter safety, EMC, anti-islanding, and grid interconnection — and impose local-content rules (taux d'intégration nationale) that may require a percentage of project value to be sourced from Algerian manufacturers or assembled locally. Importers of PV inverters must comply with Algerian customs requirements including HS code classification, applicable import duties, and any conformity documentation required by the Ministry of Industry or IANOR. For utility-scale projects, a CREG generation licence is mandatory before construction.
target_standards: Loi 04-09 relative à la promotion des énergies renouvelables dans le cadre du développement durable
Loi 02-01 relative à l'électricité et à la distribution du gaz par canalisations
CREG / Ministry of Energy Solar 1000 programme and follow-on tender documentation
Local-content (taux d'intégration nationale) rules per tender specifications
Algerian Customs Tariff and import regulations
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverters exported to Algeria are classified under HS 8504.40 (static converters) for customs purposes. Chinese export documentation (CCC exemption letter, GB/T 37408-2021 test report, factory inspection certificate) is not a substitute for Algerian IANOR conformity attestation, CREG tender technical requirements, or Sonelgaz grid-connection approval. Local-content rules in Algerian tenders may require Chinese manufacturers to partner with Algerian entities or establish local assembly operations to qualify.
rows.gap: Confirm HS code classification with an Algerian customs broker. Establish whether IANOR conformity attestation or a recognised third-party IEC test certificate is required for customs clearance, import licence, or the relevant CREG tender or project-finance requirement. Review local-content rules in the applicable tender specification to determine whether partnership with an Algerian entity or local assembly is required. Engage CREG or the Ministry of Energy early for utility-scale solar projects to understand licensing timelines.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory customs compliance (HS classification, duties, conformity attestation as required); CREG generation licence mandatory for utility-scale projects; local-content requirements are tender-specific and must be confirmed in the tender documentation
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese export requires standard customs export documentation; GB/T 37408-2021 is mandatory for Chinese market but does not satisfy Algerian tender or import conformity requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Algeria is a significant and growing solar market with ambitious national capacity targets. Chinese exporters should engage a local agent, customs broker, and legal adviser to confirm current import conformity requirements, customs duties, CREG tender technical specifications, and local-content rules before the first shipment or tender bid.

source.publisher: Commission de Régulation de l'Electricité et du Gaz (CREG), Algeria

rows.requirement: CN-DZ Trade and Preferential Tariff Context for Solar Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Algeria has a trade and cooperation agreement with the European Union (the Euro-Mediterranean Association Agreement) and bilateral trade agreements with Arab League countries through the Greater Arab Free Trade Area (GAFTA). Algeria does not have a free trade agreement with China as of 2026. Standard import duties for HS 8504.40 static converters apply for Chinese-origin goods entering Algeria under the Most Favoured Nation (MFN) rate. The Algerian Customs authority (Direction Générale des Douanes — DGD) administers the tariff schedule and import controls. Algerian import regulations may include special import authorisation requirements or conformity attestation from IANOR for certain categories of electrical equipment. Importers should confirm the current MFN rate, value-added tax (TVA), and any parafiscal levies with the DGD or a licensed Algerian customs broker.
target_standards: Algerian Customs Tariff (DGD — Direction Générale des Douanes)
HS 8504.40 MFN import duty rate for static converters
Algerian VAT (TVA) on imports
Loi de finances (annual finance law — may adjust duty rates or import conditions)
GAFTA (Greater Arab Free Trade Area) — applicable for Arab League country origin goods only
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter exports to Algeria use standard export customs procedures under MOFCOM / GACC rules. China certificate of origin (Form A or self-certification) does not confer preferential tariff treatment in Algeria as no GSP or FTA rate applies for Chinese-origin goods under the Algerian tariff schedule as of 2026. Algeria has historically applied import restrictions and licensing regimes to manage foreign exchange and support domestic industry; exporters should confirm whether any specific import licence or foreign exchange pre-approval is required for the applicable product category.
china_standards: China MOFCOM / GACC export customs rules
Certificate of Origin (Form A or self-certification)
rows.gap: No FTA rate is available for Chinese-origin solar inverters entering Algeria. Confirm the current MFN import duty rate for HS 8504.40, TVA rate, and any parafiscal levies with DGD or a licensed Algerian customs broker. Determine whether any import licence, foreign exchange pre-approval, or conformity attestation is required before the goods can be cleared. Include Algeria import duty and TVA in the landed-cost model. Verify whether Algerian government programmes offer duty exemptions for renewable-energy equipment on a project basis.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory to comply with Algerian customs import duty, TVA, and any applicable import licensing obligations; duty exemptions are project-specific and must be confirmed with DGD
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese export customs compliance is mandatory; no FTA preference applies for Algeria

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] No China–Algeria FTA applies as of 2026. Confirm current MFN import duty for HS 8504.40, TVA, any import licence requirement, and project-level duty exemptions with DGD or a licensed customs broker before pricing the export.

rows.requirement: IANOR NA / IEC 62109 Inverter Safety Certification
rows.target_requirement: IANOR (Institut Algérien de Normalisation) is Algeria's national standardisation body, responsible for developing and adopting Algerian standards (NA prefix). Algeria adopts IEC standards as Algerian National Standards (NA) through the IANOR framework. Solar PV inverters are expected to meet IEC 62109-1 (safety for power converters — general requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (safety for PV inverters) as referenced in IANOR conformity and project procurement specifications. Algerian legislation on product conformity and technical regulation may require IANOR conformity attestation or an accepted third-party test report from an accredited laboratory against these standards before a product can be placed on the Algerian market or connected to the Sonelgaz grid. Large-scale solar project tenders (Solar 1000, follow-on programmes targeting 3 000–4 000 MW) typically require IEC-evidenced safety documentation as part of tender technical specifications.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 as referenced in IANOR NA framework (safety for power converters — general requirements)
IEC 62109-2 as referenced in IANOR NA framework (safety for PV inverters)
Algerian technical regulations on product conformity and electrical equipment market surveillance
Solar 1000 / follow-on tender technical specifications (CREG / Ministry of Energy)
IEC 61727 (PV systems — characteristics of the utility interface)
rows.china_equivalent: China solar PV inverter safety is governed by GB/T 37408-2021 (technical specification for PV grid-connected inverters) and supplementary NB/T 32004 or T/CPIA group standards. GB/T 37408-2021 covers protection and safety functions but is not identical in scope or test methodology to IEC 62109-1/2. A Chinese test report against GB/T 37408-2021 is not directly accepted by IANOR or an Algerian project owner or tender authority without supplementary IEC-route evidence.
rows.gap: Obtain or commission test reports from an accredited laboratory against IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, and prepare a declaration of conformity or IANOR conformity-mark application for the Algeria market. Chinese GB/T 37408-2021 test data may support a gap analysis but does not replace IEC-route evidence. Confirm with IANOR or the Algerian project owner or tender authority whether IANOR conformity attestation is mandatory before sale, import, or grid connection.

mandatory_status.target: De facto mandatory for tender participation in CREG / Ministry of Energy solar auctions; IANOR NA / IEC 62109 compliance is the expected technical route; project-owner or lender requirements may make it contractually mandatory
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for grid-connected PV inverters under Chinese market surveillance; GB/T 37408-2021 is the applicable standard

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408-2021 test evidence is a useful starting point but does not satisfy the IANOR / IEC 62109 route expected for Algeria. Commission IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 testing from an accredited laboratory before shipment or tender submission.

rows.requirement: EMC and Anti-Islanding Requirements for PV Inverters in Algeria
rows.target_requirement: Algeria applies IEC-based electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) standards through IANOR NA adoption. Power conversion equipment including PV inverters should meet relevant IEC 62920 (EMC requirements for PV power generation systems) and IEC CISPR conducted and radiated emission limits. Anti-islanding protection must comply with IEC 62116 (utility-interconnected PV inverters — test procedure for islanding prevention measures). Sonelgaz and OS may specify additional EMC and protection requirements in their grid-connection technical specifications and project-specific requirements. Algeria's large-scale solar tender specifications (Solar 1000 programme and follow-on tenders) typically reference IEC EMC and anti-islanding standards as eligibility criteria.
target_standards: IEC 62920 (EMC requirements for PV power generation systems) as referenced via IANOR NA
IEC 62116 (utility-interconnected PV inverters — test procedure for islanding prevention measures)
IEC CISPR 11 / CISPR 32 conducted and radiated emission limits as adopted via IANOR NA
IEC 61000 series immunity standards as referenced in Sonelgaz grid-connection specifications
Solar 1000 / follow-on tender EMC and protection eligibility requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverters are tested for EMC under GB/T 17626 series and GB 4824 (CISPR 11 equivalent), and for anti-islanding under GB/T 37408-2021 and NB/T 32004 which incorporate anti-islanding test methodologies. These Chinese test results give useful technical evidence but are not equivalent to IANOR NA / IEC 62920 or IEC 62116 certifications for the Algeria market. Chinese inverters exported to tender programmes will require IEC 62116 test certificates specifically.
rows.gap: Supplement Chinese EMC test evidence with IEC 62920-route tests from an accredited laboratory. Obtain an IEC 62116 anti-islanding test certificate for the specific inverter model, as this is commonly required by Sonelgaz and Algeria solar tender technical specifications. Verify project-specific EMC and protection requirements with the Sonelgaz network operator or the project engineer before final equipment selection.

mandatory_status.target: IEC 62116 anti-islanding certification is de facto mandatory for Solar 1000 / CREG tender participation and Sonelgaz grid connection; IEC 62920 EMC evidence is expected by project owners and lenders
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory under Chinese CCC or voluntary certification routes; GB 4824 / GB/T 17626 are the applicable EMC standards; anti-islanding under GB/T 37408-2021

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese EMC test reports are technically comparable but not directly accepted as IANOR NA / IEC 62920 conformity evidence for Algeria. Obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 62920 EMC test certificates from an accredited laboratory before tender submission or grid connection.
